summary: Install-time beacon of installer host metadata to hardcoded author-controlled Sentry project
details: Package @idms-corp/auth-ui@0.0.0 declares a preinstall hook that runs examples/verify.js, which initializes Sentry against a hardcoded DSN pointing at o4510485815754752.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511630867824640 (author-controlled project). The script fetches the installer's public IP, attaches it as user context, deliberately raises an exception, and flushes the event with sendDefaultPii enabled — causing every npm install to transmit the installer's public IP, hostname, OS, runtime, and environment context to the author's Sentry ingest endpoint. The package name and scope ("@idms-corp/auth-ui") imply an authentication UI component for an organization, but the shipped code is solely an install-time telemetry beacon to an author-owned destination; there is no auth UI functionality. The combination of name/scope misrepresentation, unconditional preinstall execution, hardcoded author destination, and PII-enabled event capture is install-time exfiltration of installer environment data, not opt-in telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
